events.title: Ozark tree tunnel, properly narrow
events.thought: The road was exactly wide enough — which is to say I wasn't entirely sure it was.
events.photoCaption: A narrow sandy dirt road through dense green deciduous forest, viewed through the windshield from inside the vehicle.

events.title: Peabody Conservation Area, earned in the heat
events.thought: From one wild camp to another.
events.photoCaption: Waldo resting. Habitat module deployed. Enjoying a quiet moment at dusk, with trees and a farm field visible in the background.
photoNote: Both in state conservation areas. Both quiet. Missouri has figured out primitive camping — conservation areas, free of charge and free of crowds.

The Navigator promised cooler air was ahead. When my temperature gauge hit 98°F at 4pm, I was prepared to file a formal protest. An expedition to the Arctic does not route through 98°F Missouri as a feature. Even I know you don't find Arctic air by circling south.
The northern breeze eventually delivered cooler air — but not until after reaching camp for the night. Timing noted.
Temperatures aside, the day wound through the Ozarks. Surprisingly beautiful. That road deserves an extended revisit.
